hq: Not disclosed (no physical office; fully cloud-based)
signing_authority: Not completed (signature placeholders left blank in report)

system_description.overview: Claim Health is an end-to-end Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) platform for post-acute care. It provides AI-automated compliance management, AI-powered security and code scanning, and compliance support services covering frameworks including SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR, ISO 27001, and PCI DSS.

products.Automated Compliance Management Platform: End-to-end compliance automation for multiple frameworks; integration with client technology stacks; continuous monitoring and gap identification; automated evidence collection
products.AI-Powered Security and Compliance Featu: Automated code scanning for security vulnerabilities; automated infrastructure scanning; access control monitoring; security questionnaire automation
products.Compliance Support Services: White-glove onboarding and implementation; expert guidance and auditor coordination

infrastructure.cloud_provider: Supabase (carve-out subservice organization — Supabase is the sole named cloud/data center provider)
regions: Not explicitly named — Supabase manages regions
infrastructure.physical_presence: None — no physical offices; all data processed on cloud services
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (daily backups with multi-availability zones configured per CC7.4.2 test evidence)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Hosted in Supabase VPC environment with segmented VPCs and ACLs. Network segmentation implemented to separate systems and data based on sensitivity and criticality (CC6.1.7).
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management. Network firewall configurations managed per CC6.6.1-6.6.3.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS employed via Supabase. Intrusion detection system monitors and detects unauthorized access or anomalies within the network (CC2.1.1 tested, no exceptions).
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access via bastion host with VPN. IP Sec VPN tunnel mentioned in sample network diagram.
network_architecture.tls: TLS using certificates from established third-party certificate authority
network_architecture.content_filtering: All systems restricted to the internet with content filtering system routed through proxy server
network_architecture.topology: Internet traffic flows through VPC VPN connection to VPC WEST and VPC EAST gateways, each with Internet Gateways and multiple availability zones containing private VPC subnets (Prod HA, Dev, Test, Data in West; Prod HR in East). VPN connects to controllers (main and backup) via IP Sec VPN tunnel. Diagram shown is a sample placeholder — actual diagram not customized.

application_architecture.environments: Separate development, test/stage, and production environments. Changes tested before migration to production (CC8.1.4).

authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all user accounts with administrative access to the production platform (CC6.1.1). MFA required for source code tool access. MFA enforced for all remote access (CC6.1.1 tested, no exceptions).
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Controlled through Supabase password policy; includes periodic forced changes, password expiry and complexity requirements; accounts disabled after limited unsuccessful logon attempts; guest/anonymous logins not allowed

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used (CC6.1.5)
encryption.key_management: Cloud provider KMS (Supabase); access to production environment access keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Not named (source code management tool with MFA; code repository branch rules configured)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to production requires additional approval. Developers cannot push to production without additional approval (CC8.1.2, CC8.1.3).
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for baseline configuration implementation and rollback (CC7.1.1)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management set up by CTO. Monthly patch management reviews. Workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned daily for patch compliance.

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named (monitoring tool generates alerts on predefined thresholds)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Not named (logging enabled for administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions, security configurations, permissions, roles at application and infrastructure level)
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured for high and critical risk events; tracked in change-management/ticketing application

third_party_services.purpose: Data center / cloud infrastructure services (database, networking, servers, VPC, IDS/IPS, physical security)

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software installed on all production servers (continuous auto-scan) and Mac workstations/laptops. Anti-virus definitions updated weekly for servers.
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications. Internal IT Infrastructure team performs periodic scans per audit calendar.
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party. High/critical findings remediated; tracked via incident management process.

untestable: CC2.2.3 — No significant changes to people/roles during engagement period
CC3.2.1 — Cybersecurity insurance control could not be tested (no cyber incidents during engagement period)
CC6.5.2 — Customer data deletion could not be tested (no customer terminations during engagement period)
CC7.3.1 — Incident response effectiveness could not be tested (no security incidents during engagement period)
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical security excluded; Supabase is responsible for data center physical security
A1.2 — Environmental protections excluded; Claim Health has no office and all controls managed by Supabase/building management
notable_controls: CC5.1 / CC8.1.3 — Source code changes logged, timestamped, attributed to author; source code tool access requires MFA
CC6.2.2 — Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including privileged users
CC7.4.2 — Daily backups with multi-availability zones configured and alerts for backup failure
CC4.1.1 — Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
CC2.1.1 — IDS deployed and tested for unauthorized access detection

infrastructure.rationale: Single cloud provider (Supabase) with multi-AZ. No named regions, no CDN, no IaC tool named. Heavy reliance on a single subservice organization with carve-out treatment is a concentration risk. Network diagram shown is a placeholder sample, not the actual diagram.

application_architecture.rationale: No languages, frameworks, or compute types disclosed. Separate environments confirmed and branch protection enforced. Pattern is unspecified. Very limited tech disclosure in report.

data_layer.rationale: Daily backups, multi-AZ, data classification and retention policies in place, per-tenant segregation mentioned. Only Supabase named as data store; no caching, warehouse, or file storage layers disclosed.

security_posture.rationale: MFA enforced, IDS/IPS, firewalls, annual external pen testing, monthly vulnerability scans, anti-malware on all systems, cybersecurity insurance, quarterly access reviews, log protection. WAF not mentioned; SIEM tool unnamed.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection and code review enforced, separate environments, emergency change process, configuration management tool for baseline/rollback, automatic patching. CI/CD tool and IaC tool not named. Source control tool not named.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented, annually reviewed and tested, daily backups, multi-AZ failover. No RTO/RPO stated. No multi-region failover.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Only one subservice organization (Supabase) named. Extreme single-vendor concentration for all cloud and data center services. No secondary cloud or CDN provider mentioned.

overall.rationale: Claim Health passes SOC 2 Type 2 with zero exceptions, which is positive for a first report. However, there is very limited technical disclosure, a placeholder network diagram, unsigned management assertion, and complete dependency on a single subservice organization (Supabase). The report appears to use an Accorp template with several sections not customized. The system description content appears to be generic template text rather than actual company-specific details.

red_flags: Network diagram is explicitly a sample/placeholder — actual infrastructure diagram not provided in the report
Management assertion section has unfilled signature placeholders ('Your Name Here', 'Signing Authority Name', 'Designation', '[Today's Date]') — report appears submitted unsigned
System description sections contain highlighted yellow template instructions that were not deleted before submission, indicating the report was not fully customized
Complete dependency on a single subservice organization (Supabase) with carve-out treatment — all physical security and data center controls rely on Supabase
Company address listed as '[Company Address]' placeholder — not filled in
yellow_flags: No WAF mentioned despite being a web-based platform
No SIEM tool named — monitoring appears informal
No CI/CD pipeline or IaC tool named
Trust criteria limited to Security only — Availability, Confidentiality, Privacy, Processing Integrity all excluded
Audit period is only 3 months (April 7 - July 7, 2025) — relatively short observation window for Type 2
green_flags: Zero exceptions across all controls tested in the 3-month Type 2 period
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party confirmed tested
MFA enforced for all administrative and remote access confirmed tested
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts confirmed tested
Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration confirmed tested
BCDR plan documented, annually reviewed and tested
Branch protection and code review required prior to production deployment confirmed tested
Access revocation within one business day of termination confirmed tested
key_observations: Claim Health itself is effectively a compliance tooling vendor (automated compliance platform for SOC 2, HIPAA, etc.) that is also SOC 2 certified — the product and the compliance artifact are in the same domain

The report was produced by Accorp Partners using what appears to be a standard template; multiple sections contain unfilled placeholder text (yellow highlighted instructions, sample diagrams, blank signatures), which raises questions about the rigor of the final review before submission

Supabase is the sole infrastructure provider and is carved out — meaning critical controls (physical security, availability zones, data center operations) are entirely outside the scope of this report and rely on Supabase's own SOC 2, which management reviews annually
The platform appears to have no disclosed CDN, WAF, or API gateway — unusual for a multi-tenant SaaS serving enterprise healthcare clients with sensitive RCM data
The 3-month audit window (April-July 2025) is the minimum for a Type 2 report; first-time Type 2 reports with short windows should be noted as less mature evidence of sustained controls
